title: capability classification + probe record + doctor unlock naming (D3/D4)
action: Per D3: add exported classifyNativeTransport(evidence) -> 'native_model_override'|'native_budget_only'|'external_cli_only' (unknown/absent evidence => 'native_budget_only') to the dispatch lib, plus a version+config-scoped probe record (codex_version, flags observed via `codex features list`, accepted-override-spawn evidence from the canary) stored beside doctor-attest with the same validity legs. Per D4: doctor gains an informational row that NAMES the unlock (features.multi_agent_v2 + hide_spawn_agent_metadata=false) when classification is native_budget_only and the binary ships the flag; bee never writes the user's codex config. New scripts/test_native_probe.mjs covers classification table + record validity legs; wire it into commands.verify and the verify manifest. ADVISOR-R2 Δ2 (binding): the classification record is a SEPARATE gitignored runtime file (e.g. .bee/native-transport-probe.json), NEVER doctor-attest.json (its 3 legs cannot see codex config changes). Validity legs: codex version + repo identity + a 4th leg hashing the probed codex feature/config scope (features.multi_agent_v2, hide_spawn_agent_metadata, tool_namespace) captured at probe time; any config-scope change invalidates the verdict back to native_budget_only. Add the file to the onboarding gitignore block if not already covered by a .bee wildcard.

truths: verdict invalidated by codex version change or flag-state change (attest pattern)
doctor only names the unlock, never applies it

verify: node scripts/test_native_probe.mjs && node scripts/test_verify_manifest.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs

trace.outcome: Added exported classifyNativeTransport(evidence) (pure, dispatch-guard.mjs, D3a-authoritative logic: external_cli_only<=>multi_agent===false; native_model_override<=>multi_agent!==false AND multi_agent_v2===true AND override_spawn_accepted===true) + version+config-scoped native-transport-probe record (writeNativeTransportProbe/readNativeTransportClassification, bee.mjs, 3 validity legs mirroring g22-3 attest, config_scope_hash covers all 4 verdict-determining flags per Delta2-amended) + D4 doctor informational row naming the unlock; scripts/test_native_probe.mjs (29 checks) wired into commands.verify and passing the verify manifest guard.

deviations.detail: Touched .gitignore, skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs, and skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs — none were in the cell's declared files list. Required because the cell's own action text says 'Add the file to the onboarding gitignore block if not already covered by a .bee wildcard' (confirmed not covered) — onboard_bee.mjs's GITIGNORE_BLOCK_PATTERNS is the single source the real .gitignore block is rendered from, and test_onboard_bee.mjs keeps a second, independently-hand-duplicated GITIGNORE_PATTERNS_FOR_HASH list specifically to catch drift between the two; both had to be updated or the drift guard would (and did, when checked) fail red.

The cell's files list named 'skills/bee-hive/templates/bin/bee.mjs', which does not exist. The real mirror-source path (confirmed byte-identical to .bee/bin/bee.mjs before this cell, and the one scripts/test_dispatch_prepare.mjs already imports) is 'skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs' (no bin/ subdirectory). Edited the real path; both copies verified byte-identical after every edit.

R3 names exactly one reader, readNativeTransportClassification(root). To keep dispatch-guard.mjs's documented zero-I/O pure-decision-core contract intact, the reader (which needs fs reads plus live `codex --version`/`codex features list` subprocess calls for its validity legs) lives in bee.mjs alongside the writer (writeNativeTransportProbe) and the D4 doctor row, mirroring the g22-3 doctor-attest pattern exactly (same file, same style). Only the genuinely pure classifyNativeTransport(evidence) lives in dispatch-guard.mjs, keeping that module's 'ZERO I/O' comment honest. Several supporting functions (doctorCodexVersion, doctorCodexFeaturesList, nativeTransportProbePath, nativeTransportConfigScopeHash, writeNativeTransportProbe, doctorNativeTransportUnlock) were exported from bee.mjs beyond the one literally-named reader, so cnt-5's future canary writer and this cell's own test file can reuse them without duplicating parsing/hashing logic.

Mid-cell, discovered two AUTHORITATIVE advisor decisions logged during this cell's own execution window that were not yet folded into plan.md's Advisor conditions section or the original dispatch prompt: decision c0cba64e (D3a, classification triggers: evidence gains a base `multi_agent` flag; external_cli_only <=> multi_agent===false is the ONLY external trigger; native_model_override requires multi_agent!==false AND multi_agent_v2===true AND override_spawn_accepted===true — the first cap's classifyNativeTransport used a simpler {multiAgentAvailable, overrideSpawnAccepted} shape that did not gate on multi_agent_v2 at all) and decision 760e9b05 (Δ2-amended: config_scope_hash must cover all FOUR verdict-determining flags {multi_agent, multi_agent_v2, hide_spawn_agent_metadata, tool_namespace} — the first cap's hash only covered two ad-hoc *_enabled keys). Reworked classifyNativeTransport's logic/evidence field names to match D3a exactly, changed writeNativeTransportProbe to derive config_scope automatically from a single evidence object (removing a separately-passable configScope parameter, eliminating the classification/hash drift risk two independent parameters created), and updated the live-recheck key names accordingly. Re-verified everything green before this second cap. This is a genuine correction to already-implemented logic, not a net-new addition — recorded per rule 1 (bug found in touched code -> auto-fix).

Mid-cell, .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs (cnt-1's file, not in cnt-2's scope) was observed with literal unresolved git-merge-conflict markers already committed on main, breaking every bee.mjs CLI invocation repo-wide for several minutes. Not touched (out of scope, multi-session etiquette) — self-resolved by the concurrent cnt-1 worker before this cell's verify/cap steps ran.

A decision log entry (b296a0cf) surfaced mid-cell suggesting cnt-2's claim may have been reassigned to a different session (6d0892b6). Flagged to the orchestrator before committing rather than proceeding unilaterally; orchestrator independently verified session 6d0892b6 was stale (~15h, no branch/worktree/live hold) and confirmed this session's ownership per the live-claim guard (GH #20) before authorizing commit.

trace.reopened_reason: Mid-cell correction: original classifyNativeTransport/config_scope_hash predates authoritative decisions c0cba64e (D3a) and 760e9b05 (Delta2-amended) logged during this cell's own execution window; reworked to match, re-verified green, re-capping with accurate outcome/deviations.
